description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a single curved handle extending from the neck to the shoulder. The body is decorated with intricate black-figure artwork in the style of ancient Greek pottery, depicting human figures in various poses, possibly mythological or ceremonial scenes. The neck and handle are a deep, matte black, contrasting with the warm, ochre-toned body. A band of geometric meander patterns encircles the shoulder, while the lower body features detailed narrative scenes. The surface appears smooth and well-preserved, with no visible chips or cracks. The overall form is elegant and balanced, making it suitable for display in a refined setting.
description: A tall, narrow-necked Greek-style ceramic vase with a single handle and detailed black-figure artwork depicting human scenes. The vase features a smooth, ochre body with black decorative motifs and a matte black neck and handle.
description_view_0: The vase's front shows a reclining figure and a standing figure in black-figure style, with a prominent handle and geometric border near the neck.
description_view_1: This view highlights the back of the vase, showing the handle's attachment and a single figure riding a horse, with less visible narrative detail.
description_view_2: The side view displays two standing figures interacting over a vessel, with the handle to the left and the geometric border clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view features a seated figure holding a staff and another standing figure, with the handle on the right and the artwork wrapping around the body.
attribute_pattern: narrative black-figure scenes with geometric borders
